common.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete? This action cannot be undone.

shell.topbarSubtitle: Configure, test, and operate self-hosted agents

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Review agents, retrieval, tool calls, and runtime protection so you know whether this environment is ready for launch testing.

help.agentReadiness: At least one enabled agent is required before you can test or invoke the runtime.
help.invokeSignal: Number of agent invocations in the last 24 hours. A value of 0 usually means no Playground or external API test has run yet.
help.retrievalSignal: Number of knowledge retrieval attempts in the last 24 hours. After binding a knowledge base, ask a grounded question in Playground to verify this increases.
help.circuitHealth: Circuit breakers protect external services. Healthy means no service is currently blocked because of repeated failures.
help.totalRequests: Total requests entering the agent runtime, including Playground and external API calls.
help.retrieval: RAG retrieval count. It shows whether agents are querying knowledge sources when they need business facts.
help.toolCalls: Number of HTTP or built-in tool calls made by agents. Use it to confirm backend integrations are being used.
help.avgLatency: Average knowledge retrieval latency. If it is high, review top_k, chunk size, vector model, or reranker settings.
help.avgLLMLatency: Average model call latency. Provider speed, context length, and tool rounds all affect this.
help.circuitBreaker: Closed means normal calls; Open means calls are temporarily blocked after repeated failures; Half Open means the system is probing recovery.
help.usage: Token usage roughly reflects your model bill — it does not include a currency conversion since providers price tokens differently.

setupCta.title: Get your first agent live in three steps
setupCta.description: Connect an AI model, pick a template, and start chatting.

usage.title: Token Usage (Last 30 Days)
usage.noData: No usage data yet. Statistics will appear automatically once conversations start.

conversations.title: Conversations
conversations.description: Browse what customers have been asking your agents and how they responded — no technical jargon, just the conversation.
conversations.noSessions: No conversations yet. They will show up here once a customer starts chatting with an agent.
conversations.selectSessionHint: Select a conversation on the left to view the full transcript.
conversations.noMessages: This conversation has no messages yet.

agents.title: Agent Management
agents.name: Agent Name
agents.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this agent?

help.capabilitySource: This is the source of truth for what the agent can access at runtime. Shortcut bindings in Integrations write back here; they do not create a second config system.
help.knowledgeCap: Select knowledge sources this agent can search. During chat, the LLM decides when to call the knowledge retrieval tool based on the user's question.
help.workflowCap: Select workflows this agent can start. Workflow names, descriptions, and steps are exposed to the LLM so it can decide when a flow should begin.
help.toolCap: Select external APIs or built-in tools this agent can call. Tool names, descriptions, and parameter schemas are exposed as function calling capabilities.
help.resourceDescription: This is not a separate trigger rule. It is the runtime capability description shown to the LLM. Prefer maintaining the resource's own name, description, and schema.
help.advancedInstruction: Normally leave this empty. Use it only when this specific agent needs extra rules for the bound resource, such as requiring user confirmation first.

workflows.title: Workflow Management
workflows.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this workflow?
workflows.deleteInUseTitle: This resource is used by {{count}} agent(s)
workflows.deleteInUseBody: Deleting it will make these agents unable to use this capability.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.description: Upload documents, maintain fast-answer entries, test retrieval, and bind knowledge sources to agent capabilities.
knowledge.supportedFormats: TXT / MD / PDF / DOCX / CSV / XLSX
knowledge.ingestionStandardValue: Text first, table fields preserved, OCR scanned files first
knowledge.question: Question
knowledge.questionPlaceholder: What documents are required for residence registration?
knowledge.autoCreateSource: Leave empty to auto-create from filename
knowledge.uploadStandardDesc: Default single-file limit is 50MB. DOCX extracts headings, paragraphs, and tables before chunking. TXT/MD supports UTF-8, GB18030, and UTF-16. CSV/XLSX should use a header row. PDFs should contain selectable text; OCR scanned files first.
knowledge.emptySources: No knowledge sources yet. Upload a document or create a source first.
knowledge.emptyChunks: This source has no chunks yet
knowledge.deleteConfirm: Delete this knowledge source and all of its chunks?
knowledge.deleteInUseTitle: This resource is used by {{count}} agent(s)
knowledge.deleteInUseBody: Deleting it will make these agents unable to use this capability.

help.page: When an agent answers a knowledge question, Aezab searches the bound sources first: fast KV, BM25 keyword, and vector retrieval run together, results are fused and optionally reranked, then the LLM answers with that context.
help.sourceType: Choose how the material should be organized: Document for manuals and policies, FAQ for standard Q&A, KV for exact facts like phone numbers or prices, Structured Table for CSV/XLSX rows.
help.domain: A domain is a retrieval namespace. Agents only search sources bound to their capabilities. Use separate domains when business lines should not retrieve each other's content.
help.chunkCount: How many searchable chunks this source contains. Too few chunks can miss facts; too many can increase retrieval cost.
help.ingestionStandard: Use selectable text when possible. DOCX extracts paragraphs and tables; CSV/XLSX should have a header row; scanned PDFs need OCR first.
help.source: Select a source to append the file there, or leave it empty to create a new source from the filename.
help.file: Supported formats: TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX, CSV, XLSX. PDFs should contain selectable text; OCR scanned files first.
help.chunkSize: Approximate characters per chunk. The default 500 works for most support documents; long policy text can use a larger value.
help.chunkOverlap: Characters repeated between adjacent chunks so answers are not cut at boundaries. The default 50 is usually enough.
help.entityKey: The lookup key for fast KV answers, such as support phone or refund window. Exact hits can return stable facts without heavy generation.
help.faq: FAQ stores standard question-answer pairs. User wording does not need to match exactly; retrieval uses similarity.
help.search: This tests retrieval only, not the full agent answer. Use Playground to test the complete runtime path.

modelStatus.notDownloadedDesc: The vector model ({{model}}, ~2GB) has not been downloaded yet. It needs to be downloaded before uploading documents; other features remain available while it downloads.
modelStatus.downloadingDesc: Downloading the vector model... users in mainland China are automatically routed through a mirror. You can leave this page; the download continues in the background.
modelStatus.uploadWarning: The vector model is not ready yet; uploads will only be searchable via keyword retrieval until it finishes. Downloading it first is recommended.

tools.title: Tool Management
tools.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this tool?
tools.deleteInUseTitle: This resource is used by {{count}} agent(s)
tools.deleteInUseBody: Deleting it will make these agents unable to use this capability.

skills.title: Skill Management
skills.managedInfo: These skills are auto-managed by agent capabilities. Edit them from the Agent page.
skills.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this skill?

llmConfigs.title: LLM Configuration Management
llmConfigs.baseUrlHelp: The web address of the model service. Auto-filled when you pick a provider above.
llmConfigs.apiKeyHelp: The secret key from your provider's account page. Stored encrypted on the server.
llmConfigs.modelHelp: Not sure? The default value usually works.
llmConfigs.temperatureHelp: How random the answers are. Lower is more precise and consistent, higher is more creative. Default works for most cases.
llmConfigs.topPHelp: Another way to control randomness, alongside Temperature. Usually left at the default.
llmConfigs.maxTokensHelp: The maximum length of a single answer. Higher allows longer replies but costs more and is slower.
llmConfigs.timeoutHelp: How long to wait for the model before giving up. Increase this if you see timeout errors.
llmConfigs.testFillRequired: Fill in the base URL and model name before testing.
llmConfigs.testNeedsKey: Enter the API key again before testing this connection.
llmConfigs.presetHint: Pick a provider below to auto-fill its connection details, or choose Custom for any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
llmConfigs.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this config?

playground.title: Playground
playground.checkLlmConfigHint: You can check this on the LLM Configs page (test connection).

agentTemplates.selectTemplateHint: Pick a built-in template to stamp out a ready-to-use agent.
agentTemplates.agentNamePlaceholder: Leave empty to use the template name
agentTemplates.llmConfigPlaceholder: Select an LLM config (optional, defaults to system default)
agentTemplates.knowledgeOnboardingHint: This template relies on a knowledge base to answer questions. Select the sources it should search.
agentTemplates.noKnowledgeSourcesHint: No knowledge sources yet? Go upload documents on the Knowledge page first.

eventSubscriptions.title: Event Subscriptions
eventSubscriptions.name: Name
eventSubscriptions.secretPlaceholder: Leave blank when editing to keep the current secret
eventSubscriptions.eventsPlaceholder: Select or type an event type
eventSubscriptions.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this subscription?
eventSubscriptions.verificationHint: Callback requests carry X-HlAB-Event and X-HlAB-Signature headers. The signature is sha256=<hmac_sha256(secret, raw_body)> — verify it with your subscription secret.
eventSubscriptions.invalidUrl: URL must start with http:// or https://

auth.title: Sign in to Aezab
auth.setupSubtitle: It looks like this is your first time here — create an admin account to sign in to this console.
auth.errorPasswordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
auth.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
auth.serverUnreachable: Cannot reach the server. Please check that the backend is running.

step1.title: Connect an AI model — it's your agent's brain
step1.hint: Pick a provider below. Most only need an API key to get started.
step1.fillRequired: Fill in the model name and address before testing.
step1.createFailed: Failed to save the model configuration.
step1.advancedSettingsHint: Using your own relay or proxy endpoint? Override the service address here.

testErrors.authError: The API key looks wrong or isn't active yet. Double-check you copied the whole key.
testErrors.networkError: Couldn't reach that address. Check your network connection or the service address.
testErrors.otherError: The test failed. See technical details below.

dashscope.name: Qwen (DashScope)
dashscope.description: From Alibaba Cloud. Direct access in mainland China, free credits on sign-up.

zhipu.name: Zhipu GLM
zhipu.description: GLM models from Zhipu AI, strong at Chinese conversation.

minimax.name: MiniMax
minimax.description: MiniMax's OpenAI-compatible API, good at reasoning tasks.

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.description: GPT models directly from OpenAI. Needs an API key with available credit.

ollama.name: Ollama (Local, Free)
ollama.description: Runs on your own machine, no API key or internet required. Start it with docker compose --profile local-llm up -d, or use a locally installed Ollama.

custom.name: Custom
custom.description: Connect any OpenAI-compatible endpoint you already have.

fields.baseUrlHelp: The API address for your model service.
fields.apiKeyHelp: Found on your provider's account or API keys page.
fields.modelHelp: Not sure? The default value usually works.

step2.title: Create your first agent
step2.hint: Pick a template that fits your use case — it comes pre-configured and ready to use.

step3.title: "{{name}}" is ready
step3.knowledgeHint: Want it to know your business better? Upload documents on the Knowledge page.

errors.unauthorized: Your login has expired or your credentials are invalid. Please sign in again.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to perform this action.
errors.rateLimited: Too many requests, or the account has run out of quota/balance. Please try again later.
errors.serverError: The server ran into a problem. Please try again in a moment.
errors.timeout: The request timed out — the server may be downloading a model or under heavy load. Please try again later.
errors.network: Can't reach the backend service. Please check that it is running.

health.title: System Health
health.checkLlmHint: Checks whether the LLM can be reached with the currently configured API key. Costs one real LLM call.

llm.auth_error: The API key is invalid or expired. Go to the LLM Configs page and re-test the connection.
llm.rate_limited: Rate limited or out of credit. Check your provider account balance or try again later.
llm.unreachable: Couldn't reach the LLM service address. Check your network connection or the Base URL.
llm.not_configured: No default LLM is configured yet. Go to the LLM Configs page and add one, then set it as default.
llm.error: The LLM call failed. Go to the LLM Configs page and test the connection for details.
